SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.automation

Class WriteValidatorRegistry

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.automation.WriteValidatorRegistry
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class WriteValidatorRegistry extends Object implements Serializable
Registry of WriteValidator instances keyed by equipment class. The registry is consulted by ProcessAutomation.setVariableValueValidated(String, double, String) and ProcessAutomation.setValuesTransactional(java.util.Map, String) before any write is applied.

The registry supports multiple validators per equipment class (all are evaluated; the first ERROR result stops the check). Subclass dispatch walks the equipment class hierarchy, so a validator registered for a base equipment class is also invoked for any subclass that does not register its own validator.

Construct a registry with the default validator set via createDefault(); this populates validators for Compressor, Pump, Separator, ThrottlingValve, Heater, and Cooler.

Version:
1.0
Author:
Even Solbraa
See Also:

  • Field Details

    • serialVersionUID

      private static final long serialVersionUID
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • validators

      private final Map<Class<? extends ProcessEquipmentInterface>,List<WriteValidator>> validators

  • Constructor Details

    • WriteValidatorRegistry

      public WriteValidatorRegistry()
      Creates an empty registry. Use createDefault() for the standard NeqSim set.

  • Method Details

    • createDefault

      public static WriteValidatorRegistry createDefault()
      Creates a registry pre-populated with the default validators for compressors, pumps, separators, throttling valves and heaters/coolers.
      Returns:
      a registry ready for production use

    • register

      public void register(WriteValidator validator)
      Registers a validator. Multiple validators may be registered for the same equipment class; all are evaluated in registration order.
      Parameters:
      validator - the validator to register; must not be null

    • unregister

      public void unregister(Class<? extends ProcessEquipmentInterface> equipmentClass)
      Removes all validators registered for the given equipment class.
      Parameters:
      equipmentClass - the equipment class whose validators should be removed

    • getValidatorsFor

      public List<WriteValidator> getValidatorsFor(ProcessEquipmentInterface equipment)
      Returns the validators applicable to the given equipment, walking the class hierarchy. The most-specific class's validators come first; base-class validators come last.
      Parameters:
      equipment - the target equipment
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable list of applicable validators (possibly empty)

    • validate

      public WriteValidationResult validate(ProcessEquipmentInterface equipment, String propertyPath, double value, String unit)
      Runs every applicable validator against the proposed write. The first ERROR result is returned immediately; otherwise the most severe non-error result is returned (or WriteValidationResult.ok() when all validators pass).
      Parameters:
      equipment - the target equipment; never null
      propertyPath - the local property path on the equipment
      value - the proposed value
      unit - the unit of measure of value, or null for the property's default unit
      Returns:
      the aggregated validation result; never null

    • getRegisteredClasses

      public Map<Class<? extends ProcessEquipmentInterface>,Integer> getRegisteredClasses()
      Returns the equipment classes for which at least one validator is registered. Used by tests and diagnostics.
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable snapshot of the keys
